pattern: Since L only moves left and R only moves right, the strings are reachable iff their non-X letters match in order with each L not past and each R not before its counterpart.
hints: An X never disappears, so dropping the X characters from both strings must leave the same sequence of L and R letters.
An L can slide leftward only and an R can slide rightward only, which constrains where each matching letter may land.
Walk two pointers over the non-X letters of both strings: they must be the same letter, and an L in start must sit at an index >= its match while an R must sit at an index <= its match.

edge_inputs.description: X moves leftward to let R pass right
an R and L would have to cross, which is forbidden

stress.description: generate a start then apply random legal moves to build a guaranteed-reachable result, plus occasional unreachable perturbations
stress.note: legal moves keep many cases reachable; random letter flips create unreachable ones
